evals.prompt: Run EKS reconnaissance on the cluster currently configured in my kubeconfig. I'm planning an upgrade — focus on version info, compute strategy, and addon inventory.
evals.expected_output: A structured reconnaissance report for the currently-configured EKS cluster covering cluster-basics (name, region, version, platform version), compute strategy, EKS-managed add-ons with versions, and IaC tool — with upgrade-relevant observations surfaced.
expectations: The output reports the current cluster version and platform version. TODO: human review
The output identifies whether Karpenter, managed node groups, Auto Mode, or Fargate is the primary compute strategy. TODO: human review
The output lists EKS-managed add-ons with their installed versions (e.g. vpc-cni, coredns, kube-proxy). TODO: human review
The output identifies the IaC tool managing the cluster (Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, eksctl, or unknown with reasoning). TODO: human review
The output calls out at least one upgrade-relevant finding (e.g. add-on version compatibility, Karpenter version, deprecated API usage). TODO: human review

knowledge_assertions.context: Report must include a cluster basics section with cluster metadata
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-recon/SKILL.md — Step 4: Generate Report schema shows top-level cluster: key or equivalent markdown section

knowledge_assertions.context: Report must include the Kubernetes version of the cluster
Report must include the EKS platform version
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-recon/references/cluster-basics.md — platform version is a core cluster-basics field

knowledge_assertions.context: Report must include the compute strategy section
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-recon/SKILL.md — report schema includes compute: top-level key or equivalent markdown section

knowledge_assertions.context: Report must identify the primary compute strategy
Report must include the add-ons section for upgrade planning
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-recon/SKILL.md — report schema includes addons: top-level key or equivalent markdown section

knowledge_assertions.context: Report must list at least one core EKS-managed add-on
Report must name the detected compute model explicitly
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-recon/references/compute.md — detection outputs one of these strategies

knowledge_assertions.context: For an upgrade-focused recon, output must surface at least one upgrade-relevant observation
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-recon/SKILL.md — Integration with Other Workflows / Upgrade Workflow section

evals.prompt: Run EKS reconnaissance on my current cluster for a handoff document. I need to understand the IaC tooling, CI/CD setup, and deployment patterns in use.
evals.expected_output: A handoff-oriented report for the currently-configured EKS cluster that focuses on compute (nodepools / node groups / Fargate profiles), IaC provenance, and CI/CD — both workspace pipelines (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins) and GitOps (ArgoCD, Flux).
expectations: The output describes the compute topology of the cluster (node groups, Karpenter nodepools, or Fargate profiles as applicable). TODO: human review
The output identifies the IaC tool and, where possible, evidence (workspace files, stack names, module references). TODO: human review
The output reports on workspace CI pipelines (GitHub Actions / GitLab CI / Jenkins) with detection evidence. TODO: human review
The output reports on GitOps tooling (ArgoCD or Flux) including namespace and rough application / kustomization counts when available. TODO: human review

knowledge_assertions.context: Report must include compute section documenting cluster topology
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-recon/SKILL.md — report schema includes compute: top-level key or equivalent markdown section

knowledge_assertions.context: Compute section must detail the specific compute resources (nodepools, node groups, or Fargate profiles)
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-recon/references/compute.md — detection covers all compute types

knowledge_assertions.context: Report must include IaC section identifying infrastructure management tool
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-recon/SKILL.md — report schema includes iac: top-level key or equivalent markdown section

knowledge_assertions.context: IaC section must name the detected tool or state unknown
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-recon/references/iac.md — detection covers Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, eksctl, Pulumi

knowledge_assertions.context: IaC detection should include confidence level or evidence for the determination
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-recon/SKILL.md — IaC output schema includes confidence and evidence fields

knowledge_assertions.context: Report must include CI/CD section covering pipelines and GitOps
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-recon/SKILL.md — report schema includes cicd: top-level key or equivalent markdown section

knowledge_assertions.context: CI/CD section must report on workspace pipeline detection
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-recon/references/cicd.md — workspace pipeline detection covers GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins

knowledge_assertions.context: CI/CD section must report on GitOps tooling (ArgoCD or Flux) detection
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-recon/references/cicd.md — GitOps detection covers ArgoCD and Flux

knowledge_assertions.context: Detection results should use detected: true/false pattern or equivalent prose for each component
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/eks-recon/SKILL.md — report schema uses detected: boolean for each sub-component
